title: Net Income Applicable to Morgan Stanley Bar Chart
content: The bar chart displays net income applicable to Morgan Stanley for two quarters: 1Q 2025 and 1Q 2026. The bar for 1Q 2025 shows a value of $4,315 million, while the bar for 1Q 2026 shows a value of $5,567 million. The chart indicates an increase in net income from the first quarter of 2025 to the first quarter of 2026.

title: Earnings per Diluted Common Share Bar Chart
content: The bar chart presents earnings per diluted common share for two quarters: 1Q 2025 and 1Q 2026. The bar for 1Q 2025 shows a value of $2.60, while the bar for 1Q 2026 shows a value of $3.43. The chart indicates an increase in earnings per share from the first quarter of 2025 to the first quarter of 2026.

title: Net Revenues Bar Chart
content: The bar chart displays net revenues for two quarters: 1Q 2025 and 1Q 2026. The bar for 1Q 2025 shows a value of $17,739 million, while the bar for 1Q 2026 shows a value of $20,580 million. The chart indicates an increase in net revenues from the first quarter of 2025 to the first quarter of 2026.

title: Non-Interest Expenses Bar Chart
content: The bar chart presents non-interest expenses for two quarters: 1Q 2025 and 1Q 2026. The bar for 1Q 2025 is divided into two segments: $7,521 million for compensation and benefits expenses (light blue) and $4,539 million for non-compensation expenses (dark blue), totaling $12,060 million. The bar for 1Q 2026 is divided into $8,542 million for compensation and benefits expenses (light blue) and $4,929 million for non-compensation expenses (dark blue), totaling $13,471 million. The chart indicates an increase in total non-interest expenses from 1Q 2025 to 1Q 2026, with both components increasing.

title: Net Revenues by Segment Bar Chart
content: The stacked bar chart displays net revenues by segment for two quarters: 1Q 2025 and 1Q 2026. For 1Q 2025, the total net revenue is $17,739 million, broken down into $8,983 million for Institutional Securities (dark blue), $7,327 million for Wealth Management (teal), and $1,602 million for Investment Management (gold). For 1Q 2026, the total net revenue is $20,580 million, broken down into $10,721 million for Institutional Securities (dark blue), $8,519 million for Wealth Management (teal), and $1,535 million for Investment Management (gold). The chart indicates growth in net revenues across all segments from 1Q 2025 to 1Q 2026.

title: Net Income by Segment for Q1 2025 and Q1 2026
content: The stacked bar chart displays the net income applicable to Morgan Stanley by segment for Q1 2025 and Q1 2026. For Q1 2025, the total net income is $4,315 million, broken down into $2,529 million from Institutional Securities, $1,532 million from Wealth Management, and $262 million from Investment Management. For Q1 2026, the total net income is $5,567 million, broken down into $3,294 million from Institutional Securities, $2,047 million from Wealth Management, and $242 million from Investment Management. The chart visually compares the contribution of each segment to total net income across the two quarters.

title: Net Revenues by Region for Q1 2025 and Q1 2026
content: The stacked bar chart displays the net revenues by region for Q1 2025 and Q1 2026. For Q1 2025, the total net revenue is $17,739 million, broken down into $13,103 million from Americas, $2,291 million from EMEA, and $2,345 million from Asia. For Q1 2026, the total net revenue is $20,580 million, broken down into $14,591 million from Americas, $2,641 million from EMEA, and $3,348 million from Asia. The chart visually compares the contribution of each region to total net revenue across the two quarters.

title: Daily 95% One-Day Total Management VaR Distribution
content: The bar chart shows the distribution of daily 95% one-day total management VaR for the current quarter, measured in millions of dollars. The horizontal axis represents VaR ranges, and the vertical axis represents the number of days. The data shows that 5 days had VaR between $40 to $45 million, 17 days between $45 to $50 million, 20 days between $50 to $55 million, 11 days between $55 to $60 million, 5 days between $60 to $65 million, 3 days between $65 to $70 million, and 2 days between $70 to $75 million. The chart indicates the frequency of different risk levels over the quarter.

title: Daily Net Trading Revenues Distribution
content: The bar chart shows the distribution of daily net trading revenues for the current quarter, measured in millions of dollars. The horizontal axis represents revenue ranges, and the vertical axis represents the number of days. The data shows that 5 days had revenues between $0 to $25 million, 6 days between $25 to $50 million, 13 days between $50 to $75 million, 12 days between $75 to $100 million, 12 days between $100 to $125 million, 4 days between $125 to $150 million, 7 days between $150 to $175 million, 3 days between $175 to $200 million, and 1 day had revenues greater than $200 million. The chart indicates the frequency of different revenue levels over the quarter.
